topic: A student finds out their scholarship was meant for someone else
raw_output:
[story 1]

Liam stared at the acceptance letter, the glossy paper suddenly feeling heavy and alien in his hands. The words, detailing the prestigious astrophysics scholarship he had poured his entire academic life into securing, were a cruel joke. He reread the line about the recipient, the name meticulously printed in bold, and his breath hitched in his throat. A slow, creeping numbness began to spread through his limbs. He blinked, trying to force the information back into a logical framework, but the pieces refused to connect. His hands trembled, causing the letter to flutter slightly. He looked around the quiet library, the fluorescent lights buzzing overhead, suddenly seeing everything in sharp, unsettling focus—the dust motes dancing in the beams, the worn spines of textbooks, the silence amplifying the frantic hammering in his chest. He slowly folded the paper, the crisp sound echoing unnervingly loud in the stillness.

[story 2]

"Wait. Wait a minute." Maya whispered, the sound barely audible above the distant hum of the campus coffee machine. Her eyes darted from the scholarship notification on her screen to the faded photograph tucked inside the envelope—a picture of an older woman smiling warmly. A cold wash swept over her skin, making the fine hairs on her arms prickle. She leaned back in her chair, the cheap plastic groaning in protest beneath her weight. Her mouth went dry. She shook her head once, sharply, trying to dislodge the impossible reality. The world seemed to tilt slightly; the familiar geometry of the room warped. She gripped the edge of the desk until her knuckles turned white, struggling to process the sudden, crushing weight of misunderstanding settling squarely on her shoulders.

[story 3]

When Professor Albright finally explained the discrepancy, the air in the office seemed to thin out. Sarah felt a strange detachment, as if she were observing a play from a distance. She hadn't heard the words clearly at first; they had simply washed over her, a tidal wave of bewildering information. She lowered her gaze to her hands, watching the slight tremor that ran through her fingers as she tried to reconcile the official documentation with the whispered truth. A faint flush rose on her neck, quickly replaced by a chilling clarity. She opened her mouth to ask a clarifying question, but no sound emerged. Her entire focus narrowed down to a single point of utter confusion, her posture stiffening as if bracing against an invisible force.

The words, detailing the prestigious astrophysics scholarship he had poured his entire academic life into securing, were a cruel joke. He reread the line about the recipient, the name meticulously printed in bold, and his breath hitched in his throat. A slow, creeping numbness began to spread through his limbs. "Wait. Wait a minute." Maya whispered, the sound barely audible above the distant hum of the campus coffee machine. Her eyes darted from the scholarship notification on her screen to the faded photograph tucked inside the envelope—a picture of an older woman smiling warmly. A cold wash swept over her skin, making the fine hairs on her arms prickle. She leaned back in her chair, the cheap plastic groaning in protest beneath her weight. Her mouth went dry. When Professor Albright finally explained the discrepancy, the air in the office seemed to thin out. Sarah felt a strange detachment, as if she were observing a play from a distance. She hadn't heard the words clearly at first; they had simply washed over her, a tidal wave of bewildering information. She lowered her gaze to her hands, watching the slight tremor that ran through her fingers as she tried to reconcile the official documentation with the whispered truth. A faint flush rose on her neck, quickly replaced by a chilling clarity. She opened her mouth to ask a clarifying question, but no sound emerged. Her entire focus narrowed down to a single point of utter confusion, her posture stiffening as if bracing against an invisible force.
